nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Documentation for the Weather API MCP server example, covering features, Docker and local run instructions, FusionAL integration steps, tool usage examples, and notes for extending the implementation.

nodes.name: requirements.txt
nodes.summary: Minimal Python dependency manifest for the weather API example server, specifying only the packages needed to run the weather MCP tool.

nodes.name: weather_server.py
nodes.summary: Example MCP server that exposes three tools for fetching current weather, multi-day forecasts, and parsing raw weather JSON responses via an external weather API.

nodes.name: get_weather
nodes.summary: Fetches current weather conditions for a given location from an external weather API and returns structured data including temperature, humidity, and conditions.

nodes.name: get_forecast
nodes.summary: Fetches a multi-day weather forecast for a location, capping the number of days, and returning daily temperature and condition data.

nodes.name: parse_weather
nodes.summary: Parses raw weather JSON data into a structured summary with temperature, humidity, and conditions, validating the input type before extraction.

nodes.name: init.py
nodes.summary: One-time initialization script for the FusionAL project that checks Docker and Python prerequisites, verifies dependencies, and sets up the MCP directory structure on first run.

nodes.name: check_docker
nodes.summary: Verifies Docker is installed and running by executing a version check subprocess, reporting success or failure to stdout.

nodes.name: check_python
nodes.summary: Checks that the Python version meets the minimum requirement for running FusionAL and reports the result.

nodes.name: check_dependencies
nodes.summary: Verifies that required Python packages are importable and reports which are missing or available.

nodes.name: setup_docker_mcp
nodes.summary: Creates the MCP directory structure under the user's home directory, including the catalogs subdirectory, as part of first-time setup.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Orchestrates the full initialization flow: runs all prerequisite checks, reports results, conditionally runs setup steps, and prints next-step instructions.

nodes.name: execution_log.json
nodes.summary: Execution log capturing Docker command runs from the think-tank or watchdog automation, including step plans, commands issued, return codes, and dry-run stdout for MCP container management operations.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Author guidelines for building FusionAL-compatible MCP servers, specifying required conventions (tool structure, Docker configuration, single-line docstrings) and anti-patterns to avoid.

nodes.name: notion_poller.py
nodes.summary: Long-running daemon that polls a Notion database for pending MCP server build requests, forwards them to FusionAL's /generate endpoint, performs fleet health checks across registered servers, and auto-resolves stale incidents.

nodes.name: notion_call
nodes.summary: Retry wrapper for Notion API calls with configurable max retries, sleeping between attempts on API exceptions and logging failures.

nodes.name: poll_build_queue
nodes.summary: Queries the Notion build queue database for pages in 'Queued' status and dispatches each to _process_build for execution.

nodes.name: _process_build
nodes.summary: Processes a single Notion build page: extracts server metadata, posts the generation request to FusionAL, updates the Notion page with build results or failure reason, and registers the new server.

nodes.name: _fail_build
nodes.summary: Marks a Notion build page as failed by updating its status and writing a truncated error message to the page properties.

nodes.name: health_check_fleet
nodes.summary: Iterates all registered MCP servers from a Notion database and checks each one's health endpoint, delegating per-server logic to _check_server.

nodes.name: _check_server
nodes.summary: Performs a health check HTTP request against a single MCP server, tracks consecutive failure counts, updates the server's Notion status page, creates incidents on repeated failures, and auto-resolves them on recovery.

nodes.name: _get_registry_page_id
nodes.summary: Looks up a server's Notion registry page ID by name, using a warm cache when available to reduce API calls.

nodes.name: _warm_registry_cache
nodes.summary: Pre-populates the in-memory registry page ID cache by fetching all pages from the Notion server registry database at startup.

nodes.name: _auto_resolve_incidents
nodes.summary: Scans open Notion incident pages for a given server and marks them resolved with a timestamp when the server has recovered.

nodes.name: recover_interrupted_builds
nodes.summary: Finds Notion build pages stuck in 'In Progress' status (from a previous crashed run) and marks them failed so they can be retried.

nodes.name: check_fusional_health
nodes.summary: Calls the FusionAL gateway's /health endpoint and logs whether the gateway is reachable and which policy profile is active.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Entry point for the notion_poller daemon: validates environment variables, prints a startup banner, initializes Notion and HTTP clients, runs startup tasks (health check, cache warm, interrupted-build recovery), then enters a continuous polling loop.

nodes.name: setup-guide.md
nodes.summary: Five-minute quick-start guide for FusionAL covering prerequisites, server startup, building the weather example, configuring Claude Desktop, testing, and common troubleshooting scenarios across 18 sections.

nodes.name: think_tank.py
nodes.summary: Multi-agent Claude collaboration script triggered by watchdog failures, where three specialized agents (InfraAgent, LogicAgent, and a reviewer) analyze MCP infrastructure problems and produce a structured action plan.

nodes.name: TTAgent
nodes.summary: Dataclass representing a single think-tank Claude agent configuration with name, role, system prompt, and model identifier.

nodes.name: server-card.json
nodes.summary: Well-known MCP server discovery card declaring FusionAL's server identity, authentication requirements (API key), and the three exposed MCP tools: execute_code, generate_and_execute, and generate_mcp_project.
nodes.languageNotes: Served at /.well-known/mcp/server-card.json to enable MCP client auto-discovery per the emerging MCP server card spec.
